title: Modules and Mixins 🧩
description: Share behaviors horizontally across unrelated classes.

slides.title: The Limitation 🛑
slides.content:
Inheritance is strictly vertical. It requires an "Is-A" parent-child relationship.

But what if a `Bird` class and an `Airplane` class both need exactly the identical `fly` method? A `Bird` is definitely not an `Airplane`!

For horizontal sharing, you need a **Module**.

slides.title: What is a Module? 🧩
slides.content:
A Module is like a mini-class that cannot be instantiated (you can't use `.new` on it).

Its sole purpose is to hold a neat bundle of methods that can be "mixed into" absolutely any other class you want!

```ruby
module Flyable
  def fly
    puts "I am flying!"
  end
end
```

slides.title: Mixing it In
slides.content: To inject the module's code flawlessly right into a class, you just write the `include` keyword followed by the Module name.
slides.codeSnippet:
module Swimmable
  def swim
    puts "Splish splash!"
  end
end

class Fish
  include Swimmable
end

class Submarine
  include Swimmable
end

nemo = Fish.new
nemo.swim

sub = Submarine.new
sub.swim

quizData.question: Which explicit keyword perfectly sucks a Module's methods seamlessly into a Class?
quizData.explanation: The `include` keyword seamlessly essentially copies-and-pastes all the methods securely from the Module directly into the Class's walls.

slides.title: Multiple Inheritance 🧬
slides.content:
In Ruby, a Class can only inherit from ONE Parent class.

However, a Class can `include` literally as many Modules as you possibly want. This gives Ruby classes incredibly massive flexibility without creating messy family trees.

quizData.question: If you need a `Duck` class to inherit `Animal` genetics, but also have `Flyable` and `Swimmable` abilities, how would you structure it?

options.text: Inherit from `Animal`, and `include` the `Flyable` and `Swimmable` modules.

quizData.explanation: You get one Parent class for core identity (`class Duck < Animal`), but limitless Modules for versatile behaviors (`include Flyable`).
